question.text: Mr. Rao retired with a corpus of Rs. 10,00,000. If inflation is 6% per year, what will be the real value (purchasing power) of his corpus after 1 year if he keeps it in a locker at home earning 0% return?

answer.text: Approximately Rs. 9,43,396 in today's purchasing power
Rs. 10,00,000 — money does not lose value in a locker

explanation.text: **The Logic:** If inflation is 6%, the purchasing power of money decreases. Real value after 1 year = Rs. 10,00,000 / (1 + 0.06) = Rs. 10,00,000 / 1.06 = approximately Rs. 9,43,396. This means although he still has Rs. 10 lakh in the locker, it can only buy goods worth about Rs. 9.43 lakh at today's prices. **The Trap:** Money in a locker does NOT grow — it stays the same in nominal terms but loses purchasing power due to inflation. Rs. 10,60,000 would be the amount he would NEED to maintain the same purchasing power. **Cross-Exam Context:** This is also tested in Series V-A (MF Distributors) under inflation impact calculations. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** This is the most powerful example to show clients why keeping money idle is actually losing money. Calculate the real loss for their specific corpus amount — it makes the concept tangible and motivates them to invest.
